text.title: Security Testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Security Testing. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: SAST findings published once a quarter; fix backlog is 200+ items at release.
antipattern.root-cause: SAST not wired into PR pipeline.
antipattern.fix: Add semgrep / bandit / gosec to CI on every PR; gate merges on critical findings.

antipattern.symptom: log4shell-style CVE in transitive dep; audit reported zero issues.
antipattern.fix: Use osv-scanner / pip-audit / cargo audit which expand transitives by default.

antipattern.symptom: ZAP report covers /health, /login, /signup; no CRUD endpoints scanned.
antipattern.fix: Configure ZAP context with form-based or bearer auth; replay session through authenticated paths.

antipattern.symptom: Secret committed; CI flags it; rotation costs 3 days.
antipattern.root-cause: gitleaks only in CI, not pre-commit.
antipattern.fix: Add gitleaks pre-commit hook + .gitleaks.toml repo config; CI remains as defence in depth.
